problem.summary: Measure device allocation overhead, stream-ordered reuse, and scratch-buffer behavior for repeated workloads. The baseline uses per-iteration hipMalloc/hipFree or framework allocator behavior; the optimized path uses hipMallocAsync, explicit memory pools, or a custom scratch allocator with stable reuse.
outputs: allocated device buffers for a synthetic or real workload
constraints: Harness-ready; no ROCm timing or profiler evidence is claimed until a HIP run writes result artifacts
Record whether timing includes allocation calls, kernel work, synchronization, graph capture, and pool warmup
Baselines should include hipMalloc/hipFree, hipMallocAsync/hipFreeAsync with default and explicit pools when supported, and relevant framework caching allocator behavior when used
A custom allocator must document ownership, stream ordering, alignment, maximum live bytes, fragmentation behavior, and failure mode

verification.method: The harness allocates scratch buffers, launches a sentinel touch kernel, accumulates a device checksum, and synchronizes streams before comparing checksum against zero.

curation.notes: ROCm parity port from CUDA task `memory-pool-allocator`. This is a template/harness scaffold only; CUDA-origin result artifacts were intentionally not copied, and no ROCm timing, correctness, or profiler-counter evidence is claimed yet. Run on AMD hardware, attach ROCm version, hipcc flags, gfx target, and label HIP-event-only results as timing-only.
